content.full_text:
March 21, 1990

Mr. Wayne Imberi
FPPC
Box 1467
Sacramento
CA 95807

Dear Mr. Imberi:

We have discussed the matter of organizations contributing to committees by telephone, I would appreciate your written comments on this matter to avoid any possible confusion.

My understanding is that an organization can make a one time only, contribution to a committee, without being required to report the contributors to the organization. The total amount of the contribution from the organization to the committee does have to be reported. The amount, for a ballot measure is unlimited, but if that amount reaches $10,000, that figure must be reported.

In a candidate election, I understand that this is the same, but is there a limit on the amount that the organization can contribute to the committee? Again, the individual contributions to the organization do not have to be reported, but the total amount of the contribution does have to be reported.

Secondly, I understand it is acceptable for anyone to make non-monetary contributions to either the organization or the committee, for which they may or may not be reimbursed. This is different from a loan, which has an understanding that is either to be repaid or forgiven.

I would appreciate any clarifications or comments regarding these practices.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Peter Bentley
Carpinterians Against Rampant Development (CARD), A committee of Save Our City. #89145

The image shows a blank sheet of light blue paper with some visible creases or folds, likely from being handled or stored. There is no text or content on the page aside from these minor imperfections.
